title: hazardia squarrosa var. obtusa
intro: Hazardia squarrosa var. obtusa, also known as 'Mountain Mahogany', is a type of shrub native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which aid in soothing sore throats, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues. The health benefits of Hazardia squarrosa var. obtusa include its ability to boost the immune system and provide relief from colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
